meta.title: Dragon's Crystal Alarm
meta.description: Build an alarm that triggers a buzzer and red LED when it gets dark.
meta.learning_goal: Using analog sensors and digital outputs to create conditional responses
meta.code_hint: Read LDR value with analogRead(), trigger buzzer and LED when light level drops below threshold

meta.title: Dragon's Crystal Alarm
meta.description: Build an alarm that triggers a buzzer and red LED when it gets dark.
meta.learning_goal: Using analog sensors and digital outputs to create conditional responses
meta.code_hint: Read LDR value with analogRead(), trigger buzzer and LED when light level drops below threshold

walkthrough.instruction: Put the red LED on the breadboard with the long leg (anode) in row 12, column E and the short leg (cathode) in row 11, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — it's called the anode!

walkthrough.instruction: Put the 220Ω resistor on the breadboard with one leg in row 11, column D and the other leg in row 7, column D.
walkthrough.tip: The resistor limits current so the component doesn't burn out.

walkthrough.instruction: Place the buzzer with the long leg (+) in row 18, column E and the short leg (−) in row 21, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — mark it with a tiny + on the buzzer.

walkthrough.instruction: Place the photoresistor (LDR) on the breadboard with one leg in row 29, column E and the other leg in row 26, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The photoresistor changes resistance based on light — brighter light means lower resistance!

walkthrough.instruction: Put the 10000Ω resistor on the breadboard with one leg in row 26, column D and the other leg in row 22, column D.
walkthrough.tip: The resistor limits current so the component doesn't burn out.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D13 to the breadboard at row 12, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 7, column E to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a red wire from the Arduino's 5V pin to the breadboard at row 29, column D.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 22, column E to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from the breadboard at row 26, column A to Arduino pin A0.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D8 to the breadboard at row 18, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 21, column D to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.
